abandon.definition: To give up or relinquish control of, to surrender or to give oneself over, or to yield to one's emotions.

absence.definition: A state of being away or withdrawn from a place or from companionship; the period of being away.
absence.example: Absence makes the heart grow fonder.

absolute.definition: That which is independent of context-dependent interpretation, inviolate, fundamental.

abstract.definition: An abridgement or summary of a longer publication.

absorb.definition: To include so that it no longer has separate existence; to overwhelm; to cause to disappear as if by swallowing up; to incorporate; to assimilate; to take in and use up.

abundant.definition: Fully sufficient; found in copious supply; in great quantity; overflowing.
abundant.example: [W]ith their magical words they [poets] bring forth to our eyesight the abundant images and beauties of creation. вАФ Leigh Hunt, On the Realities of Imagination

academic.definition: (usually capitalized) A follower of Plato, a Platonist.

accept.definition: To receive, especially with a consent, with favour, or with approval.

access.definition: A way or means of approaching or entering; an entrance; a passage.

accelerate.definition: To cause to move faster; to quicken the motion of; to add to the speed of.

accommodation.definition: (usually a mass noun) Lodging in a dwelling or similar living quarters afforded to travellers in hotels or on cruise ships, or prisoners, etc.

accompany.definition: To go with or attend as a companion or associate; to keep company with; to go along with.
accompany.example: Geoffrey accompanied the group on their pilgrimage.

achieve.definition: To succeed in something, now especially in academic performance.

accurate.definition: Telling the truth or giving a true result; exact; not defective or faulty

acknowledge.definition: To admit the knowledge of; to recognize as a fact or truth; to declare one's belief in
acknowledge.example: to acknowledge the being of a god

account.definition: A registry of pecuniary transactions; a written or printed statement of business dealings or debts and credits, and also of other things subjected to a reckoning or review.

adapt.definition: To make suitable; to make to correspond; to fit or suit

adjust.example: Morimoto's recipes are adjusted to suit the American palate.

admire.definition: To be amazed at; to view with surprise; to marvel at.

admit.definition: To allow to enter; to grant entrance (to), whether into a place, into the mind, or into consideration
admit.example: A ticket admits one into a playhouse.

advantage.definition: Any condition, circumstance, opportunity or means, particularly favorable to success, or to any desired end.
advantage.example: The enemy had the advantage of a more elevated position.

adopt.definition: To take by choice into relationship (a child, heir, friend, citizen, etc.)

advance.definition: A forward move; improvement or progression.
advance.example: an advance in health or knowledge

affair.definition: (often in the plural) Something which is done or is to be done; business of any kind, commercial, professional, or public.

aid.example: He came to my aid when I was foundering.

agreement.definition: An understanding between entities to follow a specific course of conduct.
agreement.example: to enter an agreement;¬† the UK and US negotiators nearing agreement;¬† he nodded his agreement.

aim.definition: The pointing of a weapon, as a gun, a dart, or an arrow, or object, in the line of direction with the object intended to be struck; the line of fire; the direction of anything, such as a spear, a blow, a discourse, a remark, towards a particular point or object, with a view to strike or affect it.
aim.example: Take time with the aim of your gun.

afford.definition: To incur, stand, or bear without serious detriment, as an act which might under other circumstances be injurious;вАФwith an auxiliary, as can, could, might, etc.; to be able or rich enough.
afford.example: I think we can afford the extra hour it will take.вАГ We can only afford to buy a small car at the moment.

amount.definition: The total, aggregate or sum of material (not applicable to discrete numbers or units or items in standard English).
amount.example: The amount of atmospheric pollution threatens a health crisis.

alternative.definition: A situation which allows a mutually exclusive choice between two or more possibilities; a choice between two or more possibilities.

alarm.definition: A summons to arms, as on the approach of an enemy.

background.definition: One's social heritage, or previous life; what one did in the past.
background.example: The lawyer had a background in computer science.

balance.definition: A state in which opposing forces harmonise; equilibrium.

base.definition: Something from which other things extend; a foundation.

basic.definition: A necessary commodity, a staple requirement.
basic.example: Rice is a basic for many Asian villagers.

bear.definition: A large omnivorous mammal, related to the dog and raccoon, having shaggy hair, a very small tail, and flat feet; a member of family Ursidae.

behave.definition: To conduct (oneself) well, or in a given way.
behave.example: You need to behave yourself, young lady.

belief.definition: Mental acceptance of a claim as true.
belief.example: It's my belief that the thief is somebody known to us.

benefit.definition: An advantage; help or aid from something.
benefit.example: It was for her benefit.¬†¬† His benefit was free beer.

blank.definition: A small French coin, originally of silver, afterwards of copper, worth 5 deniers; also a silver coin of Henry V current in the parts of France then held by the English, worth about 8 pence .

block.definition: A substantial, often approximately cuboid, piece of any substance.

board.definition: A relatively long, wide and thin piece of any material, usually wood or similar, often for use in construction or furniture-making.

bore.definition: A hole drilled or milled through something, or (by extension) its diameter.

brief.definition: A writ summoning one to answer to any action.

bother.example: There was a bit of bother at the hairdresser's when they couldn't find my appointment in the book.

bound.definition: To tie; to confine by any ligature.

budget.definition: The amount of money or resources earmarked for a particular institution, activity or time-frame.

broad.definition: A shallow lake, one of a number of bodies of water in eastern Norfolk and Suffolk.

burst.definition: An act or instance of bursting.
burst.example: The bursts of the bombs could be heard miles away.

capable.definition: Able and efficient; having the ability needed for a specific task; having the disposition to do something; permitting or being susceptible to something.
capable.example: As everyone knew, he was capable of violence when roused.

capture.definition: An act of capturing; a seizing by force or stratagem.

capacity.definition: The ability to hold, receive or absorb

career.definition: One's calling in life; a person's occupation; one's profession.

celebrate.definition: To extol or honour in a solemn manner.
celebrate.example: to celebrate the name of the Most High

character.definition: A being involved in the action of a story.

charge.definition: The amount of money levied for a service.
charge.example: There will be a charge of five dollars.

charity.definition: An organization, the objective of which is to carry out a charitable purpose.

chief.definition: A leader or head of a group of people, organisation, etc.
chief.example: All firefighters report to the fire chief.

climate.definition: An area of the earth's surface between two parallels of latitude.

claim.definition: A demand of ownership made for something.

classic.definition: A perfect and/or early example of a particular style.

coach.definition: A wheeled vehicle, generally drawn by horse power.

circumstance.definition: That which attends, or relates to, or in some way affects, a fact or event; an attendant thing or state of things.

combine.example: We can't finish harvesting because our combine is stuck in the mud.

command.example: I was given a command to cease shooting.

collapse.example: She suffered a terrible collapse after slipping on the wet floor.

comfort.example: Sleep in comfort with our new mattress.

commission.definition: A sending or mission (to do or accomplish something).

community.definition: A group sharing a common understanding, and often the same language, law, manners, and/or tradition.

commit.definition: The act of committing (e.g. a database transaction or source code into a source control repository), making it a permanent change.

compete.definition: To be in battle or in a rivalry with another for the same thing, position, or reward; to contend

complain.definition: To express feelings of pain, dissatisfaction, or resentment.
complain.example: Joe was always complaining about the noise made by his neighbours.

concentrate.definition: A substance that is in a condensed form.

conduct.definition: The act or method of controlling or directing

concern.definition: That which affects oneвАЩs welfare or happiness. A matter of interest to someone. The adposition before the matter of interest is usually over, about or for.
concern.example: Judy's eyes filled with concern as she listened to the news report.

condition.definition: A logical clause or phrase that a conditional statement uses. The phrase can either be true or false.

conclude.definition: To end; to come to an end.
conclude.example: The story concluded with a moral.

concept.definition: An abstract and general idea; an abstraction.

conflict.definition: A clash or disagreement, often violent, between two or more opposing groups or individuals.
conflict.example: The conflict between the government and the rebels began three years ago.

conference.definition: The act of consulting together formally; serious conversation or discussion; interchange of views.

confirm.definition: To strengthen; to make firm or resolute.

connect.definition: (of an object) To join (to another object): to attach, or to be intended to attach or capable of attaching, to another object.
connect.example: I think this piece connects to that piece over there.

consequence.definition: That which follows something on which it depends; that which is produced by a cause.

conscious.definition: The part of the mind that is aware of itself; the consciousness.

conservative.definition: A person who favors maintenance of the status quo.

consider.example: Consider that weвАЩve had three major events and the year has hardly begun.

constant.definition: That which is permanent or invariable.

discuss.definition: To converse or debate concerning a particular topic.
discuss.example: I don't wish to discuss this further. Let's talk about something else.

display.example: The trapeze artist put on an amazing acrobatic display.

dismiss.definition: To discharge; to end the employment or service of.
dismiss.example: The company dismissed me after less than a year.

device.definition: Any piece of equipment made for a particular purpose, especially a mechanical or electrical one.
device.example: There are a number of household devices in a kitchen such as a dishwasher, a garbage disposal, or an electric can opener.

distribute.definition: To divide into portions and dispense.
distribute.example: He distributed the bread amongst his followers.

distinguish.definition: To recognize someone or something as different from others based on its characteristics.

district.definition: An administrative division of an area.

domestic.definition: A house servant; a maid; a household worker.

document.definition: An original or official paper used as the basis, proof, or support of anything else, including any writing, book, or other instrument conveying information pertinent to such proof or support.

donate.definition: To make a donation; to give away something of value to support or contribute towards a cause or for the benefit of another.
donate.example: He donated an etching from his own collection to the new art gallery.

educate.example: Wang said such changes to the Baishui glacier provide the chance to educate visitors about global warming.

doubt.definition: Disbelief or uncertainty (about something); a particular instance of such disbelief or uncertainty.
doubt.example: I have doubts about how to convert this code to JavaScript.

dramatic.definition: Of or relating to the drama.

efficient.definition: A cause; something that causes an effect

effort.definition: The work involved in performing an activity; exertion.
effort.example: It took a lot of effort to find a decent-sized, fully-furnished apartment within walking distance of the office.¬†¬† He made a conscious effort to not appear affected by the stories in the paper.

element.definition: One of the simplest or essential parts or principles of which anything consists, or upon which the constitution or fundamental powers of anything are based.
element.example: Letters are the elements of written language.

eliminate.definition: To completely remove, get rid of, put an end to.

embrace.definition: An act of putting arms around someone and bringing the person close to the chest; a hug.

emotion.definition: A person's internal state of being and involuntary physiological response to an object or a situation, based on or tied to physical state and sensory data.

employ.definition: The state of being an employee; employment.
employ.example: The school district has six thousand teachers in its employ.

emphasis.definition: Special weight or forcefulness given to something considered important.
emphasis.example: He paused for emphasis before saying who had won.

encounter.definition: A meeting, especially one that is unplanned or unexpected.
encounter.example: Their encounter was a matter of chance.

guarantee.definition: Anything that assures a certain outcome.
guarantee.example: Can you give me a guarantee that he will be fit for the match?

grant.definition: The act of granting; a bestowing or conferring; concession; allowance; permission.

hesitate.definition: To stop or pause respecting decision or action; to be in suspense or uncertainty as to a determination.
hesitate.example: He hesitated whether to accept the offer or not; men often hesitate in forming a judgment.

ignore.definition: To deliberately not listen or pay attention to.
ignore.example: A problem ignored is a problem doubled.

household.definition: Collectively, all the persons who live in a given house; a family including attendants, servants etc.; a domestic or family establishment.

identify.definition: To establish the identity of someone or something.

host.definition: One which receives or entertains a guest, socially, commercially, or officially.
host.example: A good host is always considerate of the guestвАЩs needs.

highlight.definition: An area or a spot in a drawing, painting, or photograph that is strongly illuminated.

image.definition: An optical or other representation of a real object; a graphic; a picture.
image.example: The Bible forbids the worship of graven images.

imagine.definition: A short fanfic or prompt placing a reader insert in a novel scenario with a character or celebrity.

immediate.definition: Happening right away, instantly, with no delay.
immediate.example: Computer users these days expect immediate results when they click on a link.

impact.definition: The striking of one body against another; collision.

implement.definition: A tool or instrument for working with.
implement.example: They carried an assortment of gardening implements in the truck.

impose.definition: To establish or apply by authority.

imply.definition: (of a proposition) to have as a necessary consequence
imply.example: The proposition that "all dogs are mammals" implies that my dog is a mammal

improve.definition: To make (something) better; to increase the value or productivity (of something).
improve.example: Buying more servers would improve performance.

incident.example: It was an incident that he hoped to forget.

include.definition: A piece of source code or other content that is dynamically retrieved for inclusion in another item.

increase.definition: An amount by which a quantity is increased.

indicate.definition: To point out; to discover; to direct to a knowledge of; to show; to make known.
indicate.example: The guard blew his whistle to indicate imminent departure.

income.definition: Money one earns by working or by capitalising on the work of others.

individual.definition: A person considered alone, rather than as belonging to a group of people.

industry.definition: The tendency to work persistently. Diligence.
industry.example: Over the years, their industry and business sense made them wealthy.

minor.definition: A person who is below the age of majority, consent, criminal responsibility or other adult responsibilities and accountabilities.
minor.example: It is illegal to sell weapons to minors under the age of eighteen.

monitor.definition: Someone who watches over something; a person in charge of something or someone.
monitor.example: The camp monitors look after the children during the night, when the teachers are asleep.

motivate.definition: To provide someone with an incentive to do something; to encourage.
motivate.example: The weekly staff meeting was meant to motivate employees.

negative.definition: Refusal or withholding of assents; prohibition, veto

moral.definition: (of a narrative) The ethical significance or practical lesson.

network.definition: A fabric or structure of fibrous elements attached to each other at regular intervals.

normal.definition: A line or vector that is perpendicular to another line, surface, or plane.

negotiate.definition: To confer with others in order to come to terms or reach an agreement.

notion.definition: Mental apprehension of whatever may be known, thought, or imagined; idea, concept.

nevertheless.definition: In spite of what preceded; yet.

numerous.example: There are numerous definitions of the word 'man'.

object.definition: A thing that has physical existence.

objective.definition: A material object that physically exists.

obtain.definition: To get hold of; to gain possession of, to procure; to acquire, in any way.

obvious.definition: Easily discovered, seen, or understood; self-explanatory.

operate.definition: To perform a work or labour; to exert power or strength, physical or mechanical; to act.

occasion.definition: A favorable opportunity; a convenient or timely chance.
occasion.example: At this point, she seized the occasion to make her own observation.

promise.definition: An oath or affirmation; a vow
promise.example: if I make a promise, I always stick to it;¬† he broke his promise

promote.definition: To raise (someone) to a more important, responsible, or remunerative job or rank.
promote.example: He promoted his clerk to office manager.

produce.definition: To yield, make or manufacture; to generate.

proper.definition: Something set apart for a special use.

property.example: Important types of property include real property (land), personal property (other physical possessions), and intellectual property (rights over artistic creations, inventions, etc.).

protect.definition: To keep safe; to defend; to guard; to prevent harm coming to.
protect.example: Condoms are designed to protect against sexually-transmitted diseases.

provide.definition: To make a living; earn money for necessities.
provide.example: It is difficult to provide for my family working on minimum wage.

profit.definition: Total income or cash flow minus expenditures. The money or other benefit a non-governmental organization or individual receives in exchange for products and services sold at an advertised price.

publish.definition: To issue (something, such as printed work) for distribution and/or sale.
publish.example: Most of the sketches Faulkner published in 1925 appeared in the Sunday magazine section of the New Orleans Times-Picayune.

purchase.definition: The acquisition of title to, or property in, anything for a price; buying for money or its equivalent.
purchase.example: They offer a free hamburger with the purchase of a drink.

quality.example: Quality of life is usually determined by health, education, and income.

rapid.definition: (often in the plural) a rough section of a river or stream which is difficult to navigate due to the swift and turbulent motion of the water.

quantity.definition: A fundamental, generic term used when referring to the measurement (count, amount) of a scalar, vector, number of items or to some other way of denominating the value of a collection or group of items.
quantity.example: You have to choose between quantity and quality.

range.definition: A line or series of mountains, buildings, etc.

react.definition: An emoji used to express a reaction to a post on social media.

realistic.definition: Expressed or represented as being accurate, practicable, or not idealistic.
realistic.example: A realistic appraisal of the situation.

pursue.definition: To follow urgently, originally with intent to capture or harm; to chase.

realize.definition: To make real; to convert from the imaginary or fictitious into reality; to bring into real existence
realize.example: The objectives of the project were never fully realized.

reasonable.definition: Having the faculty of reason; rational, reasoning.

recognize.definition: To match (something or someone which one currently perceives) to a memory of some previous encounter with the same person or thing.

reduce.definition: To bring down the size, quantity, quality, value or intensity of something; to diminish, to lower.
reduce.example: to reduce weight, speed, heat, expenses, price, personnel etc.

recommend.definition: To bestow commendation on; to represent favourably; to suggest, endorse or encourage as an appropriate choice.
recommend.example: The board recommends Philips, given his ample experience in similar positions.

reform.definition: The change of something that is defective, broken, inefficient or otherwise negative, in order to correct or improve it
reform.example: A major reform is needed to improve the efficiency in the factory.

signal.definition: A sequence of states representing an encoded message in a communication channel.

significance.definition: The extent to which something matters; importance
significance.example: As a juror your opinion is of great significance for the outcome of the trial.

similar.definition: That which is similar to, or resembles, something else, as in quality, form, etc.

situation.definition: The combination of circumstances at a given moment; a state of affairs.
situation.example: The United States is in an awkward situation with debt default looming.

software.definition: Encoded computer instructions, usually modifiable (unless stored in some form of unalterable memory such as ROM).

social.definition: A festive gathering to foster introductions.
social.example: They organized a social at the dance club to get people to know each other.

skill.definition: Capacity to do something well; technique, ability. Skills are usually acquired or learned, as opposed to abilities, which are often thought of as innate.

standard.definition: A principle or example or measure used for comparison.

source.definition: The person, place or thing from which something (information, goods, etc.) comes or is acquired.
source.example: The accused refused to reveal the source of the illegal drugs she was selling.

stable.definition: A building, wing or dependency set apart and adapted for lodging and feeding (and training) animals with hoofs, especially horses.
stable.example: There were stalls for fourteen horses in the squire's stables.

solution.definition: A homogeneous mixture, which may be liquid, gas or solid, formed by dissolving one or more substances.

strategy.definition: The science and art of military command as applied to the overall planning and conduct of warfare.

strength.definition: The quality or degree of being strong.
strength.example: It requires great strength to lift heavy objects.

status.definition: A personвАЩs condition, position or standing relative to that of others.
status.example: Superstition is highly correlated with economic status.

structure.definition: A cohesive whole built up of distinct parts.
structure.example: The birds had built an amazing structure out of sticks and various discarded items.

struggle.definition: A contortion of the body in an attempt to escape or to perform a difficult task.

succeed.definition: To follow in order; to come next after; hence, to take the place of.

suffer.example: Is anyone here afraid of suffering hardship?

urge.definition: A strong desire; an itch to do something.

valid.example: I will believe him as soon as he offers a valid answer.

version.definition: A specific form or variation of something.

various.definition: Having a broad range (of different elements).

vision.definition: The sense or ability of sight.

victim.definition: One that is harmedвАФkilled, injured, subjected to oppression, deceived, or otherwise adversely affectedвАФby someone or something, especially another person or event, force, or condition; in particular:
victim.example: the youngest victims of the brutal war

vital.definition: Relating to, or characteristic of life.
vital.example: vital energies; vital functions; vital actions

volunteer.definition: One who enters into, or offers for, any service of his/her own free will, especially when done without pay.

vulnerable.definition: More or most likely to be exposed to the chance of being attacked or harmed, either physically or emotionally.
vulnerable.example: It's okay to get vulnerable every now and again.

welfare.definition: Health, safety, happiness and prosperity; well-being in any respect.

widespread.definition: Affecting a large area (e.g. the entire land or body); broad in extent; widely diffused.

volume.definition: A three-dimensional measure of space that comprises a length, a width and a height. It is measured in units of cubic centimeters in metric, cubic inches or cubic feet in English measurement.
volume.example: The room is 9x12x8, so its volume is 864 cubic feet.

worthwhile.definition: Good and important enough to spend time, effort, or money on.
worthwhile.example: Doing volunteer work to help others is truly worthwhile.

witness.definition: Attestation of a fact or event; testimony.
witness.example: She can bear witness, since she was there at the time.

yield.definition: To pay, give in payment; repay, recompense; reward; requite.

attitude.definition: The position of the body or way of carrying oneself.
attitude.example: The ballet dancer walked with a graceful attitude.

available.definition: Such as one may avail oneself of; capable of being used for the accomplishment of a purpose.

associate.definition: A person united with another or others in an act, enterprise, or business; a partner.

appreciate.definition: To be grateful or thankful for.

zone.definition: Each of the five regions of the earth's surface into which it was divided by climatic differences, namely the torrid zone (between the tropics), two temperate zones (between the tropics and the polar circles), and two frigid zones (within the polar circles).

authority.definition: The power to enforce rules or give orders.
authority.example: I have the authority to penalise the staff in my department, but not the authority to sack them.

audience.definition: A group of people within hearing; specifically, a large gathering of people listening to or watching a performance, speech, etc.
audience.example: We joined the audience just as the lights went down.

aware.definition: Vigilant or on one's guard against danger or difficulty.
aware.example: Stay aware! Don't let your guard down.

award.definition: A judgment, sentence, or final decision. Specifically: The decision of arbitrators in a case submitted.

curve.definition: A gentle bend, such as in a road.
curve.example: You should slow down when approaching a curve.

cycle.definition: An interval of space or time in which one set of events or phenomena is completed.
cycle.example: the cycle of the seasons, or of the year

deadline.definition: A time limit in the form of a date on or before which something must be completed.
deadline.example: I must make this deadline or my boss will kill me!

dean.definition: A senior official in a college or university, who may be in charge of a division or faculty (for example, the dean of science) or have some other advisory or disciplinary function (for example, the dean of students).

defense.definition: The action of defending or protecting from attack, danger, or injury.

delegate.definition: A person authorized to act as representative for another; a deputy

deficit.definition: Deficiency in amount or quality; a falling short; lack.

democracy.definition: Rule by the people, especially as a form of government; either directly or through elected representatives (representative democracy).

depression.definition: In psychotherapy and psychiatry, a state of mind producing serious, long-term lowering of enjoyment of life or inability to visualize a happy future.
depression.example: I used to suffer from depression, but now I'm mostly content with my life.

deposit.definition: Sediment or rock that is not native to its present location or is different from the surrounding material. Sometimes refers to ore or gems.

derive.definition: To obtain or receive (something) from something else.

defendant.definition: Serving, or suitable, for defense; defensive, defending.

dimension.definition: A single aspect of a given thing.

dispute.definition: An argument or disagreement, a failure to agree.

discount.example: This store offers discounts on all its wares. That store specializes in discount wares, too.

disorder.definition: Absence of order; state of not being arranged in an orderly manner.
disorder.example: After playing the children left the room in disorder.

diverse.definition: Consisting of many different elements; various.

dissolve.definition: A form of film punctuation in which there is a gradual transition from one scene to the next

haul.definition: An act of hauling or pulling, particularly with force; a (violent) pull or tug.

hearing.definition: (stative) To perceive sounds through the ear.
hearing.example: I was deaf, and now I can hear.

headquarters.definition: The military installation from which troops are commanded and orders are issued; the military unit consisting of a commander and his support staff.

harbor.example: The neighborhood is a well-known harbor for petty thieves.

heritage.definition: An inheritance; property that may be inherited.

horizon.definition: The visible horizontal line or point (in all directions) that appears to connect the Earth to the sky.
horizon.example: A tall building was visible on the horizon.

humanity.definition: Mankind; human beings as a group.

ideal.definition: A perfect standard of beauty, intellect etc., or a standard of excellence to aim at.

humor.definition: The quality of being amusing, comical, funny.
humor.example: She has a great sense of humour, and I always laugh a lot whenever we get together.

hunger.definition: A need or compelling desire for food.

identical.definition: (usually pluralized) Something which has exactly the same properties as something else.

ignorance.definition: The condition of being uninformed or uneducated. Lack of knowledge or information.

index.definition: An alphabetical listing of items and their location.
index.example: The index of a book lists words or expressions and the pages of the book upon which they are to be found.

impulse.definition: A thrust; a push; a sudden force that impels.

immigrant.definition: A non-native person who comes to a country from another country in order to permanently settle there.

indication.definition: Act of pointing out or indicating.

inevitable.definition: Something that is predictable, necessary, or cannot be avoided.

infection.definition: The act or process of infecting.

incentive.definition: Something that motivates, rouses, or encourages.
incentive.example: I have no incentive to do housework right now.

infant.definition: A very young human being, from birth to somewhere between six months and two years of age, needing almost constant care and/or attention.

nationality.definition: Membership of a particular nation or state, by origin, birth, naturalization, ownership, allegiance or otherwise.

necessity.definition: The quality or state of being necessary, unavoidable, or absolutely requisite.
necessity.example: I bought a new table out of necessity. My old one was ruined.

navigation.definition: The theory, practice and technology of charting a course for a ship, aircraft or spaceship or (colloquially) road vehicle.

nomination.definition: An act or instance of nominating.

nonprofit.definition: An organization that exists for reasons other than to make a profit, such as a charitable, educational or service organization.
nonprofit.example: Employees of a nonprofit rarely make as much as greedy counterparts in the commercial world.

norm.definition: That which is normal or typical.
norm.example: Unemployment is the norm in this part of the country.

occupation.definition: An activity or task with which one occupies oneself; usually specifically the productive activity, service, trade, or craft for which one is regularly paid; a job.

obligation.definition: The act of binding oneself by a social, legal, or moral tie to someone.

optimistic.definition: Expecting the best in all possible ways.

organizational.definition: Of, relating to, or produced by an organization.

oxygen.definition: The chemical element (symbol O) with an atomic number of 8 and relative atomic mass of 15.9994. It is a colorless and odorless gas.

ownership.definition: The state of having complete legal control of something; possession; proprietorship.

panel.definition: A (usually) rectangular section of a surface, or of a covering or of a wall, fence etc.
panel.example: Behind the picture was a panel on the wall.

parameter.definition: A value kept constant during an experiment, equation, calculation or similar, but varied over other versions of the experiment, equation, calculation, etc.

parallel.definition: One of a set of parallel lines.

partnership.definition: The state of being associated with a partner.

parliament.definition: A formal council summoned (especially by a monarch) to discuss important issues.

peak.definition: A point; the sharp end or top of anything that terminates in a point; as, the peak, or front, of a cap.

penalty.example: The penalty for his crime was to do hard labor.

pension.definition: An annuity paid regularly as benefit due to a retired employee, serviceman etc. in consideration of past services, originally and chiefly by a government but also by various private pension schemes.
pension.example: Many old people depend on their pension to pay the bills.

perception.definition: The organisation, identification and interpretation of sensory information.

retail.definition: The sale of goods directly to the consumer, encompassing the storefronts, mail-order, websites, etc., and the corporate mechanisms, branding, advertising, etc. that support them.

resistance.definition: The act of resisting, or the capacity to resist.
resistance.example: the resistance of bacteria to certain antibiotics

residence.definition: The place where one lives; one's home.

reverse.example: We believed the Chinese weren't ready for us. In fact, the reverse was true.

rhythm.definition: The variation of strong and weak elements (such as duration, accent) of sounds, notably in speech or music, over time; a beat or meter.
rhythm.example: Dance to the rhythm of the music.

robot.definition: A machine built to carry out some complex task or group of tasks by physically moving, especially one which can be programmed.

royal.definition: A royal person; a member of a royal family.

ruin.definition: (sometimes in the plural) The remains of a destroyed or dilapidated construction, such as a house or castle.

route.definition: A course or way which is traveled or passed.
route.example: The route was used so much that it formed a rut.

scandal.definition: An incident or event that disgraces or damages the reputation of the persons or organization involved.
scandal.example: Their affair was reported as a scandal by most tabloids.

scenario.definition: An outline of the plot of a dramatic or literary work.

satellite.definition: A moon or other smaller body orbiting a larger one.
satellite.example: A spent upper stage is a derelict satellite.

rival.definition: A competitor (person, team, company, etc.) with the same goal as another, or striving to attain the same thing. Defeating a rival may be a primary or necessary goal of a competitor.
rival.example: Chris is my biggest rival in the 400-metre race.

tactic.definition: A maneuver, or action calculated to achieve some end.

tale.definition: An account of an asserted fact or circumstance; a rumour; a report, especially an idle or malicious story; a piece of gossip or slander; a lie.

tax.definition: Money paid to the government other than for transaction-specific goods and services.

tap.definition: A tapering cylindrical pin or peg used to stop the vent in a cask.

telescope.definition: A monocular optical instrument that magnifies distant objects, especially in astronomy.

therapy.definition: Attempted remediation of a health problem following a diagnosis, usually synonymous with treatment.

territory.definition: A large extent or tract of land; for example a region, country or district.

textile.definition: (usually in the plural) Any material made of interlacing fibres, including carpeting and geotextiles.

thereby.definition: By it; by that; by that means, or as a consequence of that.

thread.definition: A long, thin and flexible form of material, generally with a round cross-section, used in sewing, weaving or in the construction of string.

threshold.definition: The bottom-most part of a doorway that one crosses to enter; a sill.

toll.definition: Loss or damage incurred through a disaster.
toll.example: The war has taken its toll on the people.

tournament.definition: During the Middle Ages, a series of battles and other contests designed to prepare knights for war.

trail.definition: The track or indication marking the route followed by something that has passed, such as the footprints of animal on land or the contrail of an airplane in the sky.

trait.definition: An identifying characteristic, habit or trend.
trait.example: The number one personality trait I hate is hypocrisy. Why can't you be consistent!?

transaction.definition: The act of conducting or carrying out (business, negotiations, plans).
transaction.example: The transaction was made on Friday with the supplier.

transition.definition: The process of change from one form, state, style or place to another.

transmission.definition: The act of transmitting, e.g. data or electric power.

trauma.definition: Any serious injury to the body, often resulting from violence or an accident.

treaty.definition: A binding agreement concluded by subjects of international law, namely states and international organizations.

trial.definition: An opportunity to test something out; a test.
trial.example: They will perform the trials for the new equipment next week.

troop.definition: (collective) A collection of people; a number; a multitude (in general).

tutor.definition: One who teaches another (usually called a student, learner, or tutee) in a one-on-one or small-group interaction.
tutor.example: He passed the difficult class with help from his tutor.

turnover.definition: The amount of money taken as sales transacted in a given period.
turnover.example: The company had an annual turnover of $500,000.

tribe.definition: A socially, ethnically, or politically cohesive group of people.
